$comment: SHARED fixture proving src/services/claimability.ts and ui/src/lib/claimability.ts agree, byte for byte, on claimReason/derivedStatus/isClaimable. Decision 68b8bb09 (option A) added the DERIVED reason `dep-dropped`. Cascade order pinned here: terminal > in-flight > rejected > inbox-planning > unapproved > held > dep-dropped > dep-rejected > deps-pending > human-assignee > claimable. dep-dropped precedes dep-rejected (a dropped dep never self-satisfies; a rejected dep is alive and re-runnable) and both precede deps-pending (the only reason that resolves without a human). Each `todos[]` entry is a Partial<Todo> INTERSECT Partial<SessionTodo>: spread it through each suite's own mk() factory. Never add a field outside that intersection.

cases.name: terminal-done
cases.why: a done todo never re-enters the pipeline
cases.subject: T

cases.name: in-flight-claimed
cases.why: an active claim outranks every dependency gate
cases.subject: T

cases.name: own-rejected
cases.why: 80f85190 — a self-rejected leaf parks for a human, never auto-reclaims
cases.subject: T

cases.name: inbox-planning-child
cases.why: an Inbox child is planning-only even when approved (373a2d52)
cases.subject: T

cases.name: unapproved
cases.why: approvedAt is the stored DECISION gate
cases.subject: T

cases.name: dep-dropped-strands-outside-dependent
cases.why: THE F4 BUG (68b8bb09). T lives outside the dropped subtree; its dependsOn points in. Pre-change this read `deps-pending` and T was silently, permanently unclaimable.
cases.subject: T

cases.name: dep-dropped-outranks-dep-rejected
cases.why: ORDERING PIN: a dropped dep never self-satisfies; a rejected dep is alive and reset-able. Surface the dead one.
cases.subject: T

cases.name: dep-dropped-outranks-deps-pending
cases.why: ORDERING PIN: the permanent blocker wins over the wait-and-it-resolves one
cases.subject: T

cases.name: reset-dep-self-heals
cases.why: SELF-HEAL PROOF: identical graph to dep-dropped-strands-outside-dependent with D reset off `dropped`. T falls straight back to deps-pending, so `dep-dropped` is DERIVED — no column, nothing to migrate.
cases.subject: T

cases.name: dep-rejected
cases.why: a rejected dep is DISTINCT from a merely-pending one and is recoverable by reset_todo
cases.subject: T

cases.name: dangling-dep-is-not-dropped
cases.why: F3: a dep id resolving to no row is a DATA BUG, not a drop. It must NOT masquerade as dep-dropped.
cases.subject: T

cases.name: dep-accepted-not-done-satisfies
cases.why: 75f7e304 symmetry: a dep terminal for ITSELF must satisfy its dependents. Fails on the UI side until the sibling leaf unifies ui/src/lib/claimability.ts::depSatisfied — that failure is the fixture doing its job.
cases.subject: T

cases.name: human-assignee
cases.why: the agent-vs-human split is LAST: a fully-unblocked human todo is actionable, not daemon-claimed
cases.subject: T
